space.name: Education Outcome Map
space.description: A graph-friendly prototype that links education routes, professions, skills, and lifestyle implications for students.
space.compatibility_note: This export is GRC-20-friendly in structure: stable IDs, typed entities, typed relations, and attached provenance.

entities.name: Medical and Health Services Managers lifestyle profile

properties.description: Building software, systems, tools, and digital products.
Helping people through medical, clinical, or care work.
Visual, digital, and product experience work.
Strategy, finance, operations, commercial growth, and management.
Analysis, evidence, investigation, and structured problem-solving.
Writing, presenting, teaching, persuading, or coordinating.
Practical work with physical systems, tools, or visible output.
Service, support, care, guidance, and impact-oriented work.

properties.notes: Security path from a technical foundation.
Common analyst path for systems-minded business grads.
One of the clearest degree-to-role mappings in the prototype.
Common business application of economics training.
Finance-adjacent path for quantitatively strong accounting grads.
Direct market research and growth analytics path.
Digital interface path for design grads.
Clear communications-to-PR path backed by the CIP crosswalk.
People-centered path, but many counseling roles require more schooling.
Technically possible, but this is a long elite training route rather than a default outcome.
Realistic lab and technician path without more schooling.
Possible only with substantial additional training after college.
Direct site and project leadership path.
Shorter healthcare route with direct licensure path.
Primary skilled-trade path with paid training.
Fast route into HVAC technician work.
Fast fabrication and industrial trades path.

sources.label: 2020 CIP to O*NET-SOC crosswalk
sources.note: Education-to-career links use the official 2020 CIP to O*NET-SOC crosswalk where available.
